title: Theo of Golden
author: Allen Levi
blurb: Theo of Golden is a beautifully crafted story about the power of creative generosity, the importance of wonder to a purposeful life, and the far-reaching possibilities of anonymous kindness.

title: Judge Stone: A Novel
author: James Patterson and Viola Davis
blurb: The most respected citizen in Union Springs, Alabama (population 3,314), is Judge Mary Stone. She holds two responsibilities sacred: running her family farm and presiding over her courtroom. It's there she draws the most controversial case in the history of the South.

title: Project Hail Mary
author: Andy Weir
blurb: An irresistible interstellar adventure as only Andy Weir could deliver, Project Hail Mary is a tale of discovery, speculation, and survival to rival The Martian—while taking us to places it never dreamed of going.

title: The Correspondent
author: Virginia Evans
blurb: Discover the word-of-mouth hit hailed by Ann Patchett as “A cause for celebration”—an intimate novel about the transformative power of the written word and the beauty of slowing down to reconnect with the people we love.

title: My Husband's Wife: A Novel
author: Alice Feeney
blurb: My Husband’s Wife is a tangled web of deception, obsession, and mystery that will keep you guessing until the last page. Prepare yourself for the ultimate mind-bending marriage thriller and step inside Spyglass—if you dare—to experience a story where nothing is as it seems.

title: Reminders of Him: A Novel
author: Colleen Hoover
blurb: A troubled young mother yearns for a shot at redemption in this heartbreaking yet hopeful story from #1 New York Times bestselling author Colleen Hoover.

title: The Last Letter
author: Rebecca Yarros
blurb: If you’re reading this, well, you know the last-letter drill. You made it. I didn’t. Get off the guilt train, because I know if there was any chance you could have saved me, you would have.

title: Dear Debbie
author: Freida McFadden
blurb: From #1 New York Times and international bestselling author Freida McFadden comes a biting, subversive thriller about what happens when women finally choose to take justice into their own hands—with killer results.

title: The Housemaid
author: Freida McFadden
blurb: An addictive psychological thriller with a jaw-dropping twist. Perfect for fans of Ruth Ware, Lisa Jewell, and Verity.

title: The Housemaid's Secret
author: Freida McFadden
blurb: An unbelievably twisty read that will have you glued to the pages late into the night. Anyone who loves The Woman in the Window, The Wife Between Us and The Girl on the Train will be completely hooked!

title: A Court of Thorns and Roses
author: Sarah J. Maas
blurb: The sexy, action-packed first book in the #1 bestselling Court of Thorns and Roses series from global phenomenon Sarah J. Maas.

title: Sunrise on the Reaping (A Hunger Games Novel)
author: Suzanne Collins
blurb: The phenomenal fifth book in the Hunger Games series! When you’ve been set up to lose everything you love, what is there left to fight for?

title: The Outsiders
author: S. E. Hinton
blurb: The Outsiders is a dramatic and enduring work of fiction that laid the groundwork for the YA genre. S. E. Hinton’s classic story of a boy who finds himself on the outskirts of regular society remains as powerful today as it was the day it was first published.

title: The Giver
author: Lois Lowry
blurb: Life in the community where Jonas lives is idyllic. Designated birthmothers produce newchildren, who are assigned to appropriate family units. Citizens are assigned their partners and their jobs. No one thinks to ask questions. Everyone obeys. Everyone is the same. Except Jonas.

title: Throne of Glass
author: Sarah J. Maas
blurb: Enter the world of Throne of Glass with the first book in this complete, #1 bestselling series by Sarah J. Maas, author of the Court of Thorns and Roses (ACOTAR) series.

title: How to Catch the Easter Bunny
author: Adam Wallace
blurb: Start a new tradition with this bright and funny children's Easter book and get inspired to build STEAM traps of your own to pair with the readaloud! Educators, families, and children will love this wonderfully creative opportunity to have fun and celebrate the holiday together.

title: How to Catch a Leprechaun
author: Adam Wallace
blurb: Celebrate St. Patrick's Day with the #1 New York Times and USA Today Bestselling Laugh-out-loud Holiday Adventure for Kids!

title: Better Than a Touchdown
author: Jalen Hurts
blurb: From Philadelphia Eagles quarterback Jalen Hurts comes an empowering story about friendship, the power of teamwork, and achieving goals together.

title: I Love you to the Moon and Back
author: Amelia Hepworth and Tim Warnes
blurb: Show your child just how strong your love is every minute of the day with this sweet, gentle rhyme that's perfect for sharing. Now in a jacketed hardcover format!

title: The Very Hungry Caterpillar
author: Eric Carle
blurb: Follow along as one of nature’s loveliest marvels eats his way through an amazing variety of foods as he prepares to grow into a beautiful butterfly! Children will love the strikingly bold, colorful pictures against a simple text in large, clear type.

title: Forgotten Home Apothecary : 250 Powerful Remedies at Your Fingertips
author: Dr. Nicole Apelian
blurb: This is the only holistic medicine book out there where you’ll find the time-tested natural remedies our grandparents relied on, back when apothecaries hadn’t yet been replaced by pharmacies.

title: Nothing Is Impossible with God: Eleven Heroes. One God. Endless Lessons in Overcoming.
author: Shannon Bream
blurb: Shannon Bream returns with a fresh collection of biblical stories that will hearten readers who feel stuck in a spiritual rut. People in the Bible struggled the exact way we do.

title: A Navy SEAL's Bug-In Guide: How to Turn Your House into the Safest Place on Earth
author: Joel Lambert
blurb: A Navy SEAL’s Bug-In Guide will help you prepare for any kind of crisis, including the most severe ones, like an EMP, social unrest, natural disasters, martial law, or a full-blown economic meltdown. Having this DIY survival book by your side is the best lifeline you can buy for your family.

title: The King Is Coming: It's Time to Prepare for the Return of Christ
author: John Bevere
blurb: Through personal testimonies and a close examination of Scripture, John Bevere offers readers spiritual truths, insight, encouragement, and practical steps to develop a readiness shaped by faith and obedience. And as with The Awe of God, each chapter will close with his 5 P's: Passage, Point, Ponder, Prayer, and Profession.

title: If the Tree Could Speak: The Story of the Cross That Saw It All
author: Tim Tebow
blurb: In If the Tree Could Speak, New York Times bestselling author Tim Tebow invites you into the story of Jesus’ crucifixion, told from the perspective of the closest thing to Jesus on that day—the wooden cross.

title: The Let Them Theory: A Life-Changing Tool That Millions of People Can't Stop Talking About
author: Mel Robbins and Sawyer Robbins
blurb: Two simple words—Let Them—will set you free. Free from the opinions, drama, and judgments of others. Free from the exhausting cycle of trying to manage everything and everyone around you. The Let Them Theory puts the power to create a life you love back in your hands—and this book will show you exactly how to do it.

title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
blurb: No matter your goals, Atomic Habits offers a proven framework for improving—every day. James Clear, one of the world’s leading experts on habit formation, reveals practical strategies that will teach you exactly how to form good habits, break bad ones, and master the tiny behaviors that lead to remarkable results.

title: The Body Keeps the Score: Brain, Mind, and Body in the Healing of Trauma
author: Bessel van der Kolk M.D.
blurb: In The Body Keeps the Score, Dr. van der Kolk uses recent scientific advances to show how trauma literally reshapes both body and brain, compromising sufferers’ capacities for pleasure, engagement, self-control, and trust.

title: The Four Agreements: A Practical Guide to Personal Freedom
author: Don Miguel Ruiz
blurb: Based on ancient Toltec wisdom, The Four Agreements offer a powerful code of conduct that can rapidly transform our lives to a new experience of freedom, true happiness, and love.

title: The Anxious Generation: How the Great Rewiring of Childhood Is Causing an Epidemic of Mental Illness
author: Jonathan Haidt
blurb: Haidt has spent his career speaking truth backed by data in the most difficult landscapes—communities polarized by politics and religion, campuses battling culture wars, and now the public health emergency faced by Gen Z. We cannot afford to ignore his findings about protecting our children—and ourselves—from the psychological damage of a phone-based life.

title: Stripped Down: Unfiltered and Unapologetic
author: Bunnie Xo
blurb: Stripped Down is the story of how Bunnie Xo rose to the top, how she used her own wiles to reach her goals, how she knew redemption was up to her—and that no one could hand it to her—and a message to anyone who needs advice on breaking their own cycles.

title: You with the Sad Eyes: A Memoir
author: Christina Applegate
blurb: Unflinchingly honest and darkly funny, You with the Sad Eyes unveils a side of Christina Applegate we’ve never seen, forever cementing her formidable and iconoclastic legacy.

title: Getting Naked: The Quiet Work of Becoming Perfectly Imperfect
author: Valerie Brertinelli
blurb: From beloved actress and New York Times bestselling author Valerie Bertinelli, her most vulnerable book yet offering wisdom hard-won through divorce, menopause, and generational pain, with a powerful message of self-acceptance and embracing the past with compassion.

title: If I Don't Return: A Father's Wartime Journal
author: Lieutenant General (Ret.) Mark Hertling
blurb: Part battlefield memoir, part father’s journal, part meditation on the challenges of leadership, If I Don’t Return is the story of a soldier who faced death, returned home, and continued to live a life of service. ‍

title: Kids, Wait Till You Hear This!
author: Liza Minneli
blurb: Global icon Liza Minnelli shares her inspiring story: stepping out from the long shadow of a mega-star mother and legendary film director father, fighting a lifetime battle with addiction, and emerging from it all to become a once-in-a-lifetime artist.

title: Apple: The First 50 Years
author: David Pogue
blurb: In time for Apple’s 50th anniversary, CBS Sunday Morning correspondent David Pogue tells the iconic company’s entire life story: how it was born, nearly died, was born again under Steve Jobs, and became, under CEO Tim Cook, the most valuable company in the world.

title: Nobody's Girl: A Memoir of Surviving Abuse and Fighting for Justice
author: Virginia Roberts Giuffre
blurb: Nobody’s Girl is an astonishing affirmation of the late Virginia Roberts Giuffre’s unshakable will—first, to claw her way out of victimhood, and then to shine light on wrongdoing and fight for a safer, fairer world. Equal parts intimate and fierce, it is a remarkable narrative of fortitude in the face of depravity and despair.

title: The Psychology of Money: Timeless lessons on wealth, greed, and happiness
author: Morgan Housel
blurb: In The Psychology of Money, award-winning author Morgan Housel shares 19 short stories exploring the different ways people think about money and teaches you how to make better sense of one of life's most important topics.

title: The 48 Laws of Power
author: Robert Greene
blurb: Some laws teach the need for prudence ("Law 1: Never Outshine the Master"), others teach the value of confidence ("Law 28: Enter Action with Boldness"), and many recommend absolute self-preservation ("Law 15: Crush Your Enemy Totally"). Every law, though, has one thing in common: an interest in total domination. In a bold and arresting two-color backage, The 48 Laws of Power is ideal whether your aim is conquest, self-defense, or simply to understand the rules of the game.

title: How to Test Negative for Stupid: And Why Washington Never Will
author: John Kennedy
blurb: One of the most distinctive and funny politicians, Senator John Kennedy (the one from Louisiana) offers his perceptive (and hilarious) takes on the ridiculousness of political life in this scathingly witty takedown of Washington and its elite denizens.

title: The Only Living Trusts Book You’ll Ever Need: How to Make Your Own Living Trust, Avoid Probate & Protect Your Heirs
author: Garrett Monroe
blurb: Don’t leave the future of your estate up to chance. This is your toolkit to ensure your assets are managed exactly as you wish, without the interference of courts or the burden of unnecessary taxes.

title: Stop, in the Name of God: Why Honoring the Sabbath Will Transform Your Life
author: Charlie Kirk
blurb: Through Stop in the Name of God, bestselling author Charlie Kirk guides you on how to unplug, recharge, and reconnect with God, family, and yourself in a way that nurtures your soul. In a world dominated by screens and constant noise, Kirk presents the Sabbath as a radical act of resistance.

title: Unreasonable Hospitality: The Remarkable Power of Giving People More Than They Expect
author: Will Guidara
blurb: Today, every business can choose to be a hospitality business—and we can all transform ordinary transactions into extraordinary experiences. Featuring sparkling stories of his journey through restaurants, with the industry’s most famous players like Daniel Boulud and Danny Meyer, Guidara urges us all to find the magic in what we do—for ourselves, the people we work with, and the people we serve.

title: Streetwise: Getting to and Through Goldman Sachs
author: Lloyd Blankfein
blurb: From the long-tenured head of Goldman Sachs, an institution legendary for its culture of success, comes a candid memoir of global leadership in an age of extreme turbulence.

title: Never Split the Difference: Negotiating As If Your Life Depended On It―Unlock Your Persuasion Potential in Professional and Personal Life
author: Chris Voss and Tahl Raz
blurb: This international bestseller, with more than 3 million copies sold, offers a field-tested approach to high-stakes negotiations—whether in the boardroom, in your community, or at home.

title: The Sooner I Go: A Novel
author: Heather Cumiskey
blurb:
Perfect for fans of Colleen Hoover and Paige Toon, this suspenseful new adult romance follows an eighteen-year-old woman reeling from loss as she’s drawn into an unexpected attraction—one that might expose the secrets she’s tried to bury.

Seven months after her boyfriend and parents die tragically on the same night, eighteen-year-old Brynn—once an aspiring singer, now desperate for any paying gig that will keep her from ending up on the streets—sets out to rebuild her shattered world. At her new ad agency job, she’s distracted by enigmatic twenty-year-old Micah. Their enemies-to-lovers attraction ignites as both struggle with secrets that could jeopardize far more than just their budding relationship.

After things turn romantic with Brynn, Micah’s psychosis becomes harder to conceal. Living with his disorder has cost him relationships before, including with his estranged father; now he fears Brynn will become the next casualty.

Things are already complicated enough before a mystery girl from Brynn’s boyfriend’s past comes forward with threatening information and unfinished business of her own. Will she reveal Brynn’s secret—and steal Micah away from her in the process?

title: The End of My Life Is Killing Me: The Unexpected Joys of a Cancer Slacker
author: Annabelle Gurwitch
blurb:
A book of small rescues against despair, in this deftly comedic and deeply contemplative memoir, New York Times bestselling author faces life’s biggest curveball only to find resilience in the most unlikely places.

After Annabelle Gurwitch received an out-of-the blue diagnosis of Stage 4 lung cancer, an existential dread set in. Precision medicine offered a temporary reprieve—but instead of turning into a cancer warrior, Annabelle declared herself a cancer slacker. Her motto: no runs, no ribbons, no religion.

Told with her signature wit, warmth, and gimlet eye, Gurwitch draws inspiration from Greek mythology and TV comedies, Kermit the Frog and Samuel Beckett. She accidentally acquires an angel, embraces being in it “just for the sex,” and finds herself on a European van tour selling merch for a heavy metal band.

In this hilariously and deeply affecting meditation on mortality, the actress and activist illuminates life with chronic disease, inequities in care, and celebrates tiny victories, the crusty ends of baguettes, the discreet pleasure of sucking at a hobby, and the unshakable bond of female friendship. She upends the notion of living each day as if it were your last, as she discovers you can carpe too much diem, embracing, instead, the extraordinariness of the ordinary.

title: An Impossibility of Crows: A Novel
author: Kirsten Kaschock
blurb:
A story of mothers, monsters, and the science of longing

In this daring and evocative tale, Agnes Krahn, a chemist trained in Philadelphia, returns to her childhood home after the death of her father. Pennsylvania is where the Krahn family has lived for six generations—bound by twisted folk wisdom and an uncanny kinship with the crows that loom over their land.

Back in the grim farmhouse of her youth, Agnes is drawn into the strange legacy she tried to leave behind. When she discovers an abandoned nest in the barn, she becomes consumed by a scientific—and deeply personal—experiment: to breed a crow large and intelligent enough to carry her daughter, Mina, to a freedom Agnes has never known herself. As the bird grows, so does its terrifying potential—manifest in language, cunning, and a violent will of its own.

A thoroughly modern, feminist novel, this is a story of mothers and daughters, inheritance and isolation, and the thin line between care and control.

title: Simple Sabotage Field Manual: Methods to Quietly Undermine Hostile Governments, Military Forces, and Industrial Operations
author: Office of Strategic Services
blurb:
Originally produced in 1944 by the United States Office of Strategic Services (OSS)—the wartime intelligence agency that later evolved into the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA)—the Simple Sabotage Field Manual is a remarkable artifact of covert warfare.

Designed for distribution among resistance groups, sympathetic civilians, and undercover operatives in enemy-occupied territories, the manual outlines practical, low-risk ways for ordinary individuals to quietly undermine hostile governments, military forces, and industrial operations.

Unlike traditional wartime sabotage involving explosives or specialized training, this guide focuses on subtle, everyday actions—small decisions, deliberate inefficiencies, and minor disruptions that, when multiplied across a population, can significantly degrade an opponent’s productivity and morale. The manual explains how simple behaviors such as misfiling documents, creating bureaucratic delays, performing work poorly, spreading confusion, and fostering interpersonal friction can collectively hinder the functioning of an organization far more effectively than dramatic acts of destruction.

title: Mahala: A Novel
subgroup: Bruce Wagner's Pick of the Week
author: Carol Marques Barrios
blurb:
Mahala opens with Soufrière coughing ash over St. Vincent, as if the island is trying to rewrite its own birth certificate. Out of that grit comes a bargain: Jane, stretched thin, promises her next child to Kavita, her best friend, whose body keeps breaking its own heart. The baby arrives, and grows up with two mothers, two doorsteps, and a name borrowed from a monument—beautiful, heavy, impossible to ignore.

Carol Marques Barrios writes the saga like family gossip lifted to myth: sugar-mill bookkeeping, Hindi endearments, Sunday starch, hurricanes, and the quiet math of women making do. Mahala is wired for mutiny; when an arranged marriage is sprung on her at thirteen, she answers with spit and sprinting. Then the book rides the diaspora circuit—Colon, Panama, the Garment District—where love, labor, and shame all come with receipts. There are bodies on the edges of this history too: an infected ship, a mother lost, and the moral hangover of choices made “for the children.”

Ruby, her ride-or-die friend, is the steady bass line; Ormond, a Great War veteran, is the romance with weather in it. When the Depression hits, Mahala’s insistence on working—and cutting her hair anyway—lands as the thesis: survival is a craft.

title: Mahala: A Novel
author: Carol Marques Barrios
blurb:
Carol Marques Barrios is a graduate of the University of Connecticut, where she earned both her Bachelor of Science in marketing and Master of Business Administration. She lives with her husband and three daughters in Fairfield, Connecticut. Mahala is her first novel.

“From the very first page, this was a five-star read—right up until the ending. The book explores the expectations that family and society place on women, highlighting their strength and courage to carve out their own paths. It is a beautifully written, deeply moving novel that tells the powerful story of two women who rise above oppression and societal norms to discover their true potential and strength.”
—Dr. Radenka Maric, president, University of Connecticut

An Excerpt from Mahala

When Mahala stepped out of the house, she glimpsed the back of a horse

harnessed to the carriage. He was a thousand pounds of pure muscle, with a gleaming jet-black coat and a long lustrous mane. Mahala had never seen such an exquisite beast.

“Mr. Joshi, what a beautiful creature this is. He looks like he should be pulling a carriage for a queen.” Mahala beamed.

“Indeed, young miss, he will. His name is Midnight. Come, step up here.” Bharat ushered both Mahala and her mother up onto the bench seat, seated himself, and secured the reins in both hands. He guided Midnight and his carriage through the streets around her Ratho Mill neighborhood. Mahala felt proud to be driven around in such a style. She liked the gazes she received from onlookers.

When the ride was over, Mahala was sufficiently pleased and wholly entertained. She thanked Bharat and bid him goodbye. She was still buzzing with happiness as she walked inside her house alongside her mother.

“Mai, don’t you think Midnight was the most handsome horse you have ever seen?!” Mahala exclaimed, her eyes wide with excitement.

“Sweetheart, I didn’t realize that you would be so taken with the man’s horse,” Kavita said with a laugh. “What did you think of the man?”

“I like him a lot, Mai,” she said, glancing up at her mother, “I do. Will he come to visit us again soon?” Her eyes sparkled at the thought of another buggy ride.

“Yes, of course, my sweetling.” Kavita smiled, then drew Mahala over to the settee to sit together. “Mahala, you—well, you are going to marry that man. Dai and I have already made the arrangements with his family and are drawing up the marriage contract. Your father is working on it as we speak. Dearest, we couldn’t be more pleased that we were able to make this auspicious match!” Kavita declared proudly.

Mahala froze. She’d always known she’d be married off someday, no doubt to someone much like Bharat—but now? “Mai, no! I don’t want to be married yet. I thought I would have more time.” Her eyes widened. “Why are you doing this so fast?”

“Sweetheart, I . . .” Kavita brushed a long lock of hair from her daughter’s face as Mahala recoiled. “Surely, you knew this day would come. Your father and I had our marriage arranged when I was just your age. I accepted my mother and father’s decision and in time you will accept ours.”

“But I—I’m not ready!” Mahala ran out of the room. Kavita’s face drained of color.

***

For two weeks after Mahala’s outburst over the marriage arrangement, things were quiet. Peaceful, even. Mahala knew her parents were giving her time to settle down and get over her initial anger, and she, for her part, engaged in no further arguments. What they didn’t know was that she had formulated a plan to deal with Bharat Joshi herself. She shared this with no one, not even her sister. She carefully calculated how to dispose of this problem, and she didn’t want to be dissuaded by anyone, least of all good girl Emily.

***

Bharat Joshi came to call once again. As far as Mahala knew, they had said nothing to him about her resistance. Kavita escorted him to the garden. He held a large bouquet of flowers in his hand.

“Nice to see you again, Mr. Joshi,” Mahala murmured, making sure to emphasize the formal title Mr. as she addressed him.

“Good afternoon, Miss Patandine.” He extended the vibrant yellow and flaming red bouquet to her. “I brought you these Gloriosa Lilies because the striking blooms seemed appropriate for you. They remind me of your boundless energy and passion for life. Would you . . . would you do me the honor of a stroll about the grounds?” Baharat entreated.

Perfect, Mahala thought. This is going to be easier than I planned. Mahala obliged and followed him into the garden. Once they were out of Kavita’s sight, he dropped to his knee and took her hand in his.

“Mahala, since that first moment I caught a glimpse of you on the beach, I knew I wanted to know more about you. I have enjoyed getting to know you better in these past few weeks and I hope you can say the same about me.”

Mahala looked blankly back at Bharat. Part of her felt bad for what she was about to do to him. He was a nice man, just not someone she wanted to marry.

“Each week I prepare to come to visit you, I find myself dizzy with anticipation. Your beauty is unequaled by any woman I have ever known.”

Mahala rolled her eyes. She kept her lips tight as she endured his lengthy, stupid proposal.

“You make me so happy to be in your company, and I can imagine a brilliant life for us with lots of children running around at our feet. I have come to realize that you are an insightful, respectful young woman with a passion for life that I haven’t seen before in someone of your age. I hope you will be happy to know that I have secured your father’s permission, as well as my family’s, to ask you to be my bride.” Bharat beamed, with pride in his voice.

 Mahala had endured enough of this monologue. It was time for her to take action. She yanked back her hand from Bharat’s clutch. “I will not marry you!”

She spat directly into his eye and flung the flowers to the ground.

***

Bharat’s face turned scarlet red, beads of sweat breaking out on his forehead. He quickly rose to his feet, brushed off his pants, and wiped his brow, then looked up and made eye contact with someone over Mahala’s shoulder.

Her mother had caught the tail end of the disastrous scene from the gate and was now careening down the garden path toward them.

Bharat addressed her mother grimly, as if Mahala were invisible. “I don’t understand how this could have gone so badly. You and Mr. Patandine gave your consent for this match, as did my own parents. I thought our courtship was going so smoothly.” He squinted his eyes at them both. “I thought she would be happy about my proposal and excited to be engaged. Did I do something to offend her?” Kavita tried to answer, but he plowed on. “I know there are twelve years between our ages, but we could have a longer engagement. I’m willing to wait if I must.”

Mahala bristled.

“I’m so sorry, Bharat, please forgive her,” her mother interrupted, grasping Mahala’s hand. “She’s young and impulsive. I’m afraid that we’ve spoiled her a bit in allowing her to have her own way most of the time. I thought she was mature enough, but apparently I made a grievous miscalculation. I’ll talk to her.”

Kavita glared at Mahala. “Maybe we can fix this.”

Mahala ripped her hand from her mother’s and marched toward the house.

As she stormed away, she heard Bharat’s voice behind her.

“I’m sorry, too, Mrs. Patandine, but I . . . I must be on my way. Thank you as always for your hospitality.”

Mahala slammed the gate behind her.
